title: tofieldia calyculata subsp. rubra
intro: Tofieldia calyculata subsp. rubra, also known as the red bogbean, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant in the family Tofieldiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and diuretic effects, which help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as urinary problems like kidney stones and inflammation. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, providing health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, and it can also be used in tinctures and extracts for medicinal purposes.
